title: ConvertKit vs Beehiiv (2025): Which Is Better?
meta_description: Compare ConvertKit and Beehiiv: pricing, features, and which email platform suits your needs best.
h1: ConvertKit vs Beehiiv: Detailed Comparison for Email Marketers
intro: ConvertKit and Beehiiv are two popular email marketing platforms designed for content creators, but they serve different audiences and use cases. ConvertKit focuses on creators selling courses and digital products, while Beehiiv specializes in newsletter monetization and growth. Understanding their differences will help you choose the right tool for your business.
choose_a_if: You sell digital products, courses, or memberships and need built-in commerce features
You want the most affordable paid plan at $29/month to get started
You need visual automation builder and advanced subscriber tagging for email sequences
choose_b_if: You want to monetize your newsletter through sponsorships and ads with their built-in network
You need more free subscribers (2,500 vs 1,000) before upgrading to a paid plan
You prioritize analytics, segmentation, and a web reader for professional newsletter distribution

faq.a: Neither is objectively better—it depends on your goals. ConvertKit excels for course creators and e-commerce businesses, while Beehiiv is superior for newsletter publishers wanting monetization options. ConvertKit has lower customer satisfaction ratings (3.2 vs 4.4 on Trustpilot), so consider your specific needs carefully.

faq.q: Which is cheaper, ConvertKit or Beehiiv?
faq.a: ConvertKit's paid plans start at $29/month compared to Beehiiv's $39/month, making ConvertKit cheaper. However, Beehiiv offers a more generous free tier with 2,500 subscribers versus ConvertKit's 1,000, which may delay paid upgrades longer depending on your growth.

faq.q: Can I switch from Beehiiv to ConvertKit?
faq.a: Yes, both platforms allow you to export your subscriber lists and migrate to another service. The migration process varies by provider, so contact their support teams for detailed instructions. Most email platforms support standard CSV exports to facilitate switching.
